what_it_is: Two intertwined, governance-grade artifacts exist in /home/nathan/CEC-Platform. (1) The EVIDENCE-INTEGRITY policy: an engineering punchlist EI-01..EI-08 ("what to build" for fact/provenance integrity) plus the corpus provenance-discipline rules in corpus/SCHEMA.md and scripts/cec_corpus_lint.py, and the formal "Monotone-tightening law (EI-07)" in corpus/SCHEMA.md. Its load-bearing mechanisms: EI-01 corpus_state taint pin (git tree ids + live_rules/manager/adv content hashes written on every ledger append and every measurement row, scripts/cec_ledger.py); EI-02 control-vs-augmented lane A/B; EI-03 untainted-corroboration admission (scripts/cec_shadow.py); EI-05 corroboration-budget/dormancy ledger; EI-06 owner-only source revocation latch (scripts/cec_source_registry.py + cec_corpus_lint.py); EI-07 monotone-tightening law + real_anchor_ratio provenance taxonomy; EI-08 server-side signature gate (CODEOWNERS + branch protection on corpus/promoted/**, machine account nathanfraske-bot cannot self-approve). Two-zone custody: corpus/staging/** is agent-writable and compiles to ADVISORY-only; corpus/promoted/** is human-only and the ONLY source of blocking artifacts; provenance is mandatory and 'model output is NOT a source.' (2) The RESEARCH CHECKLIST: the paper-track under docs/research/ (README.md index + milestone ladder M1-M4 + PP-01..PP-13 punchlist) enforcing claim-evidence discipline: at-most-two falsifiable claims each with a named kill experiment and explicit '[CITE NEEDED] do NOT invent' cite gaps (claims.md/PP-01); a commit-timestamp-before-data preregistration of the EI-02 A/B with locked metrics, exclusions, and success criterion (prereg-control-lane.md/PP-06); negative-results.md/PP-04 written before claims to discipline them; and a no-orphan instrumentation inventory (instrumentation-inventory.md/PP-07) where 'zero rows may read hope to compute later' — every claimed number maps to a named existing/in-progress mechanism. CLAUDE.md also carries a separate hardware 'Project-specific verification checklist.'
checklist_items: RESEARCH CHECKLIST / milestone ladder (docs/research/README.md): M1 — first signed promotion (a corpus/promoted/** entry merged through the real signature gate; owner GitHub-verified review, machine account unable to self-approve, EI-08 DONE). M2 — 30-night control dataset (EI-02 control lane run to its preregistered round/night count, every round carrying a corpus_state pin and a control/augmented tag). M3 — ablation (augmented tier removed and re-run on held-out rounds). M4 — preprint (claims, negative results, prereg, instrumentation table reconciled against actual M2/M3 data; cites filled; external-replication limitation stated). 'We do not write the discussion section before M3.'

Claims discipline (docs/research/claims.md, PP-01): 'At most two claims.' Each is one falsifiable sentence, with the single experiment that would kill it, current evidence, and cite gaps. 'Nothing here is asserted that the instrumentation inventory (instrumentation-inventory.md, PP-07) does not have a named mechanism for.' Cite gaps are tagged '[CITE NEEDED]' with the instruction '(do NOT invent these).'

Preregistration checklist (docs/research/prereg-control-lane.md, PP-06): commit-timestamped BEFORE any lane-tagged round exists ('If lane-tagged data predates this commit, this preregistration is VOID'); §0 preconditions (NR-3 lever verified live in-loop, lane field + EI-01 corpus_state pin on every row, frozen inputs); §3 deterministic-by-round-index lane assignment ('not gameable by the agent'); §4 primary/secondary/derived metrics locked; §5 exclusion rules locked-before-analysis; §6 run count + minimum-analyzable thresholds; §7 one-sided success criterion locked; §8 anything not preregistered is exploratory; §9 self-evaluation limitation disclosed.

Instrumentation no-orphan checklist (docs/research/instrumentation-inventory.md, PP-07): one table mapping every claimed number -> mechanism -> where it lives -> status (DONE/BUILDING/EXISTS) -> precondition-to-flip. Rule: 'zero rows may read hope to compute later.' 'A number with no mechanism does not appear in the paper.' No-orphan audit: every metric named in claims.md and prereg-control-lane.md must appear as a row.

Negative-results checklist (docs/research/negative-results.md, PP-04): drafted BEFORE claims to discipline them; each negative cites the artifact it came from; NR-1..NR-5 + Limitations (N=1 team, single reviewer, one board family, no external replication, self-evaluation) 'must appear in the abstract, not buried.'

scripts/checklist.sh repo-hygiene gate (runs in CI): '==> corpus provenance lint (SB-13/14: no sourceless or model-sourced entries)' via cec_corpus_lint.py; '==> host test suites (AM-02 corpus anchors + prompt-tier-audit / EI-02 control-lane)' via unittest including tests.test_ei02_control_lane; policy-as-code load assertions; corpus compiler determinism (double-compile byte-identical); CL-19 extractor-eval holdout isolation ('no tool reads tests/holdout').

CLAUDE.md 'Project-specific verification checklist' (separate, hardware-domain): recurring review pass on connectors (RJ-45 8P8C, no Mini-Fit Jr on module-to-Hub links), pin allocation, ESD/PoE protection, FTP shielding, DETECT resistor code table, module sensing, shunt values, RS-485 presence, CAN termination, power netclass widths, in-repo vendored libraries (${KIPRJMOD}-relative only), BOM totals.
evidence_integrity_rules: Two-zone custody (corpus/SCHEMA.md): 'staging | corpus/staging/** | agents (any tier) | ADVISORY checks only — flag namespace ADV-<id>; can never block, never auto-sign-deny, never count toward human_signoff's blocking flags' and 'promoted | corpus/promoted/** | human only (CODEOWNERS + branch protection: owner's GitHub-verified approval) | blocking artifacts (DRC fragments, netlist assertions, keep-apart tables, scorer limits)'.

Mandatory provenance, no model sources (corpus/SCHEMA.md source block): 'source: { // provenance is mandatory; model output is NOT a source ... }' and 'rationale_trace: ledger-hash // ... lint REJECTS source fields resolving to a model artifact (already enforced)'. Enforced by scripts/cec_corpus_lint.py: 'Validates per shape (source whitelist, no model sources, Class C run refs, spec-section resolution, duplicate ids ACROSS zones)'. SOURCE_TYPES = {standard, datasheet, fab, spec, decision, measurement}.

EI-07 Monotone-tightening law (corpus/SCHEMA.md): 'Unsigned influence on EVALUATION is MONOTONE-TIGHTENING ONLY.' Raise-only (a weight <= current is noop:not_a_tightening; a negative weight is rejected:negative_would_reward); clamped at PENALTY_MAX (1000.0); 'Base gates are untouchable' (Kelvin/diff-pair routed, DRC-clean, base cec_score weights not in the unsigned channel's reach); 'the unsigned loop can only ever make the bar HARDER to clear.' 'Unsigned influence on GENERATION is UNRESTRICTED but LEDGER-ATTRIBUTED' — every move recorded (cec_fs_actuator Delta/DeltaLog, EI-01 corpus_state pin, symmetric Outcome) and rolled back unless its treatment round beats its paired uninfluenced control.

EI-01 taint pin (scripts/cec_ledger.py corpus_state(); scripts/cec_inloop_audit.py): 'pin the KNOWLEDGE STATE a round/run saw, so shadow evidence can be partitioned into knowledge-influenced vs uninfluenced' — promoted_tree/staging_tree git tree ids + live_rules_sha/manager_rules_sha/adv_set_sha content hashes 'written on every append and every measurement row.'

EI-06 owner-only source revocation (scripts/cec_corpus_lint.py): 'an entry citing a REVOKED external root is REFUSED with a NAMED reason' — 'REFUSED -- {reason} (EI-06: re-cite a current authoritative ...)'; compile-path latch refuses a PROMOTED entry's blocking artifact whose root is revoked.

EI-05 corroboration-budget/dormancy (scripts/cec_corpus_lint.py): a STAGING entry that crossed its corroboration budget (>= N control-lane appearances OR M nights, defaults CEC_EI05_N=12 / CEC_EI05_M=14) with 0 untainted corroborations / 0 judge true-positives becomes a deprecation candidate.

EI-03 untainted-only admission (scripts/cec_shadow.py): 'a staging entry earns promotion on FIELD EVIDENCE'; corroboration counts only on a control-lane round (steering OFF, EI-02), keyed by corpus_state so an entry's support comes from rounds that did not themselves depend on it.

EI-08 signature gate (claims.md / .github/CODEOWNERS): promotion enforced by 'a server-side cryptographic signature gate (CODEOWNERS + branch protection on corpus/promoted/**, with agents on a machine account that cannot self-approve)'; machine account nathanfraske-bot cannot approve owner-path PRs.

Dark-seat contract (claims.md): a verifier/spec-conformance seat reading an empty/unsigned corpus/promoted/ must declare itself dark and contribute only to a QUORUM verdict typed as lower-confidence — 'never a flat full verdict' (QUORUM-not-FULL owner rule, 2026-06-11).

Status honesty / commit-ordering (docs/research/README.md): 'Everything here is commit-timestamped on purpose. A preregistration (PP-06) is only worth the bytes if it lands in git BEFORE the data it governs exists. Read git log -- docs/research/ to check ordering before trusting any "we predicted X" statement.'
how_enforced: Enforced by a layered combination of CI, scripts, governance tests, server-side branch protection, and documented discipline — NOT a single hook. (1) CI: .github/workflows/kicad-checks.yml runs './scripts/checklist.sh' (step 'Repo hygiene checklist') and a unittest step including tests.test_ei02_control_lane, tests.test_prompt_audit_fixes, tests.test_auditor_dispatch, and the corpus anchor suites; plus an in-container 'cec_corpus_compile.py fixtures' validation. (2) scripts/checklist.sh (run in CI and locally as the disaster-recovery gate) invokes scripts/cec_corpus_lint.py ('no sourceless or model-sourced entries'), the host test suites, policy-as-code validation, corpus-compile determinism (byte-identical double compile), and holdout isolation. (3) scripts/cec_corpus_lint.py is the provenance linter (exit nonzero on errors; EI-05/06 latches loaded in main()); cec_corpus_compile.py carries the EI-06 revocation latch on the compile path. (4) Governance unit tests: tests/test_corpus_governance.py covers EI-05/06/07 (revocation poisons citing entries, corroboration->dormant, monotone-tightening documented). (5) EI-07 monotone-tightening is additionally enforced in running code by apply_findings in scripts/cec_inloop_audit.py and the cec_fullstack mirror (raise-only, PENALTY_MAX clamp, untouchable base gates), and EI-01 corpus_state pins are written on every ledger append/measurement row by scripts/cec_ledger.py. (6) Server-side: .github/CODEOWNERS + branch protection on corpus/promoted/**, cec-policy.json, tests/golden/, corpus/SCHEMA.md make promotion a cryptographic owner-review gate that the bot account cannot self-approve (EI-08). (7) The RESEARCH CHECKLIST half (claims/prereg/negative-results/instrumentation) is enforced largely by documented discipline + git commit-timestamp ordering (preregistration VOID if data predates the commit; check 'git log -- docs/research/'), with the deterministic gates and the corpus_state partition as the technical guardrails against self-evaluation. NOTE: there is no dedicated pre-commit/pre-push git hook wiring these in (.claude/hooks/ contains only session/context/idle-queue hooks); ops/hooks/pre-push exists per the prompt-audit but was not inspected for integrity enforcement.
verbatim_excerpts: docs/research/README.md: 'It is deliberately separate from the engineering punchlists (the EVIDENCE-INTEGRITY list EI-01..EI-08 and the PAPER-TRACK list PP-01..PP-13) — those say *what to build*; the docs here say *what we will claim, how each claim could be killed, and which number comes from which mechanism*.'

docs/research/README.md: 'Everything here is **commit-timestamped on purpose**. A preregistration (PP-06) is only worth the bytes if it lands in git BEFORE the data it governs exists. Read `git log -- docs/research/` to check ordering before trusting any "we predicted X" statement.'

docs/research/README.md milestone ladder: '- **M1 — first signed promotion.** A `corpus/promoted/**` entry merged through the real signature gate (owner GitHub-verified review, machine account unable to self-approve — EI-08 DONE). ... - **M2 — 30-night control dataset.** The EI-02 control lane (PP-06) run to its preregistered round/night count, with every round carrying a `corpus_state` pin and a control/augmented tag. ... - **M3 — ablation.** ... - **M4 — preprint.** Claims, negative results, prereg, and the instrumentation table reconciled against the actual M2/M3 data; related-work cites filled ...'

docs/research/claims.md: 'The one-page claims document. **At most two claims.** Each is one falsifiable sentence, with the single experiment that would kill it and the current evidence and cite gaps. Nothing here is asserted that the instrumentation inventory (`instrumentation-inventory.md`, PP-07) does not have a named mechanism for.'

docs/research/claims.md cite-gap discipline: '**Cite gaps (do NOT invent these).** - [CITE NEEDED] Prior art on signature/attestation-gated data or model-update pipelines ... - [CITE NEEDED] Prior art on provenance/lineage tracking for ML training or agentic memory ...'

docs/research/instrumentation-inventory.md: 'One table mapping **every number the paper will claim** → **the mechanism that produces it** → **its current status**. The rule: **zero rows may read "hope to compute later."** Every row has a named, existing or in-progress mechanism. A number with no mechanism does not appear in the paper.'
docs/research/instrumentation-inventory.md no-orphan audit: '**No metric in the claims or prereg lacks a row; no row reads hope-to-compute-later.**'

docs/research/prereg-control-lane.md: '**Status: PREREGISTERED. This document must be commit-timestamped in git BEFORE any lane-tagged round exists.** Check `git log -- docs/research/prereg-control-lane.md` against the first appearance of a `lane` field ... If lane-tagged data predates this commit, this preregistration is VOID and the experiment must be re-designed and re-registered.'

docs/research/prereg-control-lane.md §3: '**Every Nth round is a signed-only control; the rest are augmented.** ... The assignment is **deterministic by round index**, not random — so it is auditable and not gameable by the agent (the agent cannot choose which rounds are controls).'

docs/research/negative-results.md: 'Drafted **before** the claims were finalized, on purpose: negative results discipline claims. Every claim in `claims.md` was written to be survivable against the items below. If a claim cannot survive a negative result here, it does not belong in the paper. These are *real, observed* negatives from this project — not hypothetical risks. Each cites the artifact it came from.'

corpus/SCHEMA.md zones table: '| **staging** | `corpus/staging/**` | agents (any tier) | ADVISORY checks only — flag namespace `ADV-<id>`; can never block, never auto-sign-deny, never count toward `human_signoff`'s blocking flags | / | **promoted** | `corpus/promoted/**` | human only (CODEOWNERS + branch protection: owner's GitHub-verified approval) | blocking artifacts (DRC fragments, netlist assertions, keep-apart tables, scorer limits) |'

corpus/SCHEMA.md source block: '"source": {  // provenance is mandatory; model output is NOT a source / "type": "standard|datasheet|fab|spec|decision|measurement", / "ref": "...", "date": "YYYY-MM-DD" }, / "rationale_trace": "ledger-hash", // ... lint REJECTS source fields resolving to a model artifact (already enforced)'

corpus/SCHEMA.md: '## Monotone-tightening law (EI-07 — stated as law; `apply_findings` already enforces it) ... 1. **Unsigned influence on EVALUATION is MONOTONE-TIGHTENING ONLY.** ... a NEGATIVE weight — which would *reward* a bad metric — is rejected outright (`rejected:negative_would_reward`). ... **Base gates are untouchable.** ... **the unsigned loop can only ever make the bar HARDER to clear.**'

corpus/SCHEMA.md: '2. **Unsigned influence on GENERATION is UNRESTRICTED but LEDGER-ATTRIBUTED.** ... The price of the freedom is **attribution**: every unsigned generation move is recorded (the `cec_fs_actuator` `Delta`/`DeltaLog`, the EI-01 `corpus_state` pin on the ledger row, the symmetric `Outcome` win/loss/overturn record) and is **rolled back** unless its treatment round beats its paired uninfluenced control.'

scripts/cec_corpus_lint.py header: 'cec_corpus_lint -- provenance discipline for the two-zone corpus (SB-13/14 + CL-01/03/05/06). ... Validates per shape (source whitelist, no model sources, Class C run refs, spec-section resolution, duplicate ids ACROSS zones) ... Dependency-free; wired into scripts/checklist.sh so it runs in CI with no KiCad. Exit nonzero on errors; warnings do not fail the build.'
scripts/cec_corpus_lint.py EI-06: 'an entry citing a REVOKED external root is REFUSED with a NAMED ...' / err(f"{where}: REFUSED -- {reason} (EI-06: re-cite a current authoritative ...")
scripts/checklist.sh: 'printf '==> corpus provenance lint (SB-13/14: no sourceless or model-sourced entries)\n' ... python3 "$CEC_SCRIPTS_DIR/cec_corpus_lint.py" || status=1'

scripts/checklist.sh host-suite leg: 'run them in the checklist too so a regression cannot pass the local disaster-recovery gate (CLAUDE.md WSL-ephemeral policy warns against relying on manual runs).' ... unittest includes tests.test_ei02_control_lane; on failure: 'FAIL: a host suite is red (corpus AM-02 anchor or a prompt-audit / EI-02 regression)'

scripts/cec_ledger.py: 'EI-01: pin the KNOWLEDGE STATE a round/run saw, so shadow evidence can be partitioned into ...' and '"corpus_state": corpus_state(live_rules),  # EI-01: knowledge state at append time'

docs/research/claims.md C1 (one-sentence falsifiable claim): 'A corpus-governed agentic loop in which promotion is enforced by a server-side cryptographic signature gate (CODEOWNERS + branch protection on `corpus/promoted/**`, with agents on a machine account that cannot self-approve) makes every piece of evidence exactly partitionable into knowledge-influenced vs uninfluenced rounds via a per-round `corpus_state` pin, AND causes a verifier seat whose authority derives from unsigned knowledge to declare itself *dark* rather than emit a confident verdict from un-ratified material.'
